reasoning: The market was at 64% on May 1st. First-time game development projects have a high failure rate due to underestimating the final polish and balancing phases, especially for a game with 10-30 levels. However, the use of Godot for a 2D tower defense game is highly feasible, and having a dedicated artist (@TobiasVyseri) who has already completed many assets removes a major bottleneck. The project is a satiric callback to Flash games, suggesting a focused, modest scope. The update on March 6 added a "minimum quality standard," reducing the chance of a cheap technicality win, which might slightly depress the true probability of success. The developer's self-admitted slow start in programming (taking days to position a rectangle) highlights early learning curve friction, but a full year (until Dec 31, 2025) is typically sufficient to overcome this for a basic tower defense game. I am heavily anchoring on the 64% market estimate but adjusting slightly downward to 62% because first-time devs almost universally overrun their schedules, and the strict quality/reception rule adds a layer of difficulty.
